weeks.title: Week 1 — Vite setup + React core concepts
weeks.description: Understand how React thinks. Components, JSX, props, and state are everything.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Scaffold your project with Vite

tasks.text: Run: npm create vite@latest my-shop -- --template react
Run: npm install, then npm run dev — confirm app opens in browser
Explore the folder structure: src/, public/, index.html, vite.config.js
Understand: what index.html does, what main.jsx does, what App.jsx does
Install Tailwind CSS: follow tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/vite exactly
Confirm Tailwind works: add className="text-blue-500" to a heading and check color

days.resource: Resource: vitejs.dev for Vite docs · tailwindcss.com/docs for Tailwind installation

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: JSX and components

tasks.text: Learn: JSX is not HTML — className not class, htmlFor not for, self-closing tags
Learn: a component is just a function that returns JSX
Learn: component names must start with uppercase (Button not button)
Build: Button, Card, Badge components in a src/components/ folder
Use them in App.jsx — compose them together like HTML elements
Style with Tailwind utility classes — no separate CSS file needed

days.resource: Resource: react.dev/learn — the new official React docs. Far better than any course. Start at "Your First Component".

tasks.text: Learn: props are how a parent passes data down to a child component
Learn: destructure props in the function signature — ({title, price}) not (props)
Learn: the children prop — what renders between opening and closing component tags
Build: ProductCard component that receives name, price, imageUrl, category as props
Render a static list of 3 ProductCards in App.jsx with different props each

days.label: Day 4 · Thursday
days.title: State with useState

tasks.text: Learn: what state is — data that changes, and when it changes the UI re-renders
Learn: const [value, setValue] = useState(initialValue)
Learn: never mutate state directly — always call the setter function
Build: a counter component with increment/decrement buttons
Build: a controlled text input — state mirrors input value in real-time
Build: a simple toggle (show/hide a div) using boolean state

days.resource: Mental model: Every time you call a setter, React re-runs your component function and redraws the UI. State is the memory between renders.

tasks.text: Learn: render a list with .map() — every item needs a unique key prop
Learn: conditional rendering — {condition && } and ternary operator
Learn: event handlers — onClick, onChange, onSubmit — always camelCase
Build: product list rendered with .map() from an array of objects
Build: a search input that filters the displayed products in real time
Push project to GitHub — commit after every feature you build

weeks.askPrompt: Explain React state (useState) to me like I am a complete beginner — with a simple analogy and a short practical code example

weeks.title: Week 2 — useEffect + React Router v6
weeks.description: Add side effects and multi-page navigation. Your app starts feeling real.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: useEffect — side effects in React

tasks.text: Learn: useEffect runs after render — for fetching, subscriptions, timers
Learn: the dependency array — [] runs once, [value] runs when value changes
Learn: cleanup function — returned from useEffect, runs before next effect or unmount
Build: fetch products from jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts inside useEffect
Build: loading state (show spinner) and error state (show error message)

days.resource: Note: In Week 3 you'll replace useEffect fetching with TanStack Query — which handles loading/error/caching far better. This week is just to understand the underlying mechanism.

tasks.text: Install: npm install react-router-dom
Learn: wrap app in BrowserRouter in main.jsx
Learn: Routes and Route components — path="/products" element={<ProductsPage />}
Learn: Link component — replaces <a> tags for client-side navigation (no page reload)
Build: pages folder — HomePage, ProductsPage, AboutPage
Build: Navbar with Link components to each page
Learn: dynamic route — path="/products/:id"
Learn: useParams() hook — extract :id from the URL inside a component
Learn: useNavigate() hook — programmatic navigation (e.g. after form submit)
Build: ProductDetailPage — reads id from URL, fetches that product, displays it
Make each ProductCard's title clickable — navigate to /products/:id

days.label: Day 4 · Thursday
days.title: Nested routes + layouts

tasks.text: Learn: nested Route — child routes share parent layout
Learn: Outlet component — where child routes render inside a layout
Build: RootLayout with Navbar + Footer — wraps all pages via Outlet
Build: a 404 NotFoundPage — render it with path="*"
Learn: NavLink vs Link — NavLink adds active class styling automatically

days.resource: Resource: reactrouter.com/en/main/start/tutorial — official React Router tutorial, very well written

tasks.text: Learn: controlled input — value tied to state, onChange updates state
Learn: form onSubmit — always call e.preventDefault() first
Learn: managing multiple form fields with one object state
Build: a contact form — name, email, message fields with validation feedback
Build: client-side validation — show error messages if fields are empty on submit

weeks.askPrompt: Show me how to set up React Router v6 with nested routes and a shared layout component using Outlet — with a complete working code example

weeks.title: Week 3 — TanStack Query (React Query)
weeks.description: Replace all your manual fetch + useEffect code with the industry-standard data fetching library.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Why TanStack Query and setup

tasks.text: Install: npm install @tanstack/react-query @tanstack/react-query-devtools
Learn: what TanStack Query solves — caching, background refetch, loading/error states, deduplication
Wrap app in QueryClientProvider with a new QueryClient() in main.jsx
Add ReactQueryDevtools to App.jsx — open the devtools panel in browser
Create src/api/products.js — a plain fetch function that returns products array

days.resource: Resource: tanstack.com/query/latest/docs — "Quick Start" and "Queries" sections. This is the best docs page in the React ecosystem.

tasks.text: Learn: useQuery({ queryKey: ['products'], queryFn: fetchProducts })
Learn: destructure { data, isLoading, isError, error } from useQuery
Learn: queryKey is the cache key — ['products', id] for a single product
Rewrite ProductsPage to use useQuery instead of useEffect + fetch
Rewrite ProductDetailPage to use useQuery(['products', id], fetchProductById)
Watch devtools — navigate between pages, observe cache hits (no refetch)

days.label: Day 3 · Wednesday
days.title: useMutation — create, update, delete

tasks.text: Learn: useMutation for POST/PUT/DELETE — different from useQuery (not automatic)
Learn: mutate() and mutateAsync() — how to trigger the mutation
Learn: onSuccess callback — invalidate queries to refetch fresh data after mutation
Build: add product form with useMutation — POST and then invalidate ['products']
Build: delete button on ProductCard — useMutation DELETE, invalidate on success

days.resource: Key pattern: After any mutation that changes the list, call queryClient.invalidateQueries(['products']) in onSuccess. This triggers a background refetch so the UI stays in sync.

tasks.text: Learn: staleTime — how long cached data is considered fresh (default 0ms)
Learn: enabled option — only run query when a condition is true (e.g. user is logged in)
Learn: keepPreviousData (placeholderData) for smooth pagination — no flicker
Build: paginated product list — page state drives queryKey, prev/next buttons
Set staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5 on product queries — avoid unnecessary refetches

days.label: Day 5 · Friday
days.title: Custom hooks — organise your query logic

tasks.text: Learn: a custom hook is just a function that starts with "use" and calls other hooks
Build: useProducts() custom hook — encapsulates useQuery for the products list
Build: useProduct(id) custom hook — single product query
Build: useCreateProduct() and useDeleteProduct() mutation hooks
Refactor all pages to use these custom hooks — components become much cleaner

days.resource: Why it matters: Custom hooks are how senior React devs organise code. Your components should only worry about rendering — not about how data is fetched or cached.

weeks.askPrompt: Show me how to build custom React hooks for TanStack Query — useProducts, useProduct(id), useCreateProduct — with complete working code

weeks.title: Week 4 — Global state + auth context
weeks.description: Share data across components without prop drilling, and build a real auth system.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: The prop drilling problem + useContext

tasks.text: Learn: prop drilling — passing props through many layers just to reach a deep child
Learn: React Context — a way to share data globally without passing props manually
Learn: createContext(), Context.Provider, useContext() hook
Build: a ThemeContext — dark/light mode toggle accessible anywhere in the app
Apply theme class to root div based on context value — Tailwind dark: classes

days.resource: Rule: Use Context for truly global state — auth user, theme, language. For server data (products, categories), keep using TanStack Query — don't put API responses in Context.

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Auth context — current user + JWT storage

tasks.text: Build: AuthContext — holds user object, token, login(), logout() functions
Learn: store JWT in memory (useState) — safer than localStorage for auth tokens
Learn: on app load, check localStorage for persisted user info to restore session
Build: login() — call the auth API (mock for now), store token, set user in context
Build: logout() — clear token and user from state and localStorage
On app load, call GET /auth/me with the restored token to verify the session is still valid server-side — logout() if it fails
Update Navbar — show "Login" when logged out, "Hi Manoj · Logout" when logged in
Build: ProtectedRoute component — reads auth from context
If not logged in: redirect to /login using Navigate component
If logged in: render the protected child component via Outlet
Wrap admin-only routes (add product, manage orders) inside ProtectedRoute
Test: try navigating to /admin/products — confirm redirect to /login

days.label: Day 4 · Thursday
days.title: Cart state with useReducer

tasks.text: Learn: useReducer — for complex state with multiple actions (better than many useStates)
Learn: dispatch({ type: 'ADD_ITEM', payload: product })
Build: CartContext with useReducer — actions: ADD_ITEM, REMOVE_ITEM, UPDATE_QTY, CLEAR_CART
Build: CartPage — list cart items, show total price, remove button per item
Show cart item count badge in Navbar from CartContext

days.resource: When to use useReducer vs useState: If state has more than 2-3 related pieces that change together, or if next state depends on previous, use useReducer.

tasks.text: Build: LoginPage — email/password form, submit calls login mutation
On login success: store token in AuthContext, navigate to /
On login error: show error message from API response
Build: RegisterPage — name, email, password, confirm password fields
Client-side: confirm passwords match before submitting

weeks.askPrompt: Show me how to build an AuthContext in React with useContext — storing the JWT token, a user object, login and logout functions, and session persistence with localStorage

weeks.title: Week 5 — UI polish + Tailwind design system
weeks.description: Make your app look professional. Design consistency, responsiveness, and reusable components.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Tailwind deep dive — layout and spacing

tasks.text: Learn: Flexbox in Tailwind — flex, items-center, justify-between, gap-4
Learn: Grid in Tailwind — grid, grid-cols-3, col-span-2
Learn: spacing scale — p-4 = 1rem, m-8 = 2rem, gap-6 = 1.5rem
Learn: responsive prefixes — sm: md: lg: xl: — mobile-first approach
Redesign ProductsPage — 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, 3 on desktop
Make Navbar collapse to hamburger menu on mobile

days.resource: Resource: tailwindcss.com/docs — keep this open while you code. Use Ctrl+F to find any utility instantly.

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Component library with shadcn/ui

tasks.text: Learn: what shadcn/ui is — copy-paste components built on Radix UI + Tailwind
Initialize: npx shadcn@latest init in your project
Add components: npx shadcn@latest add button card input badge dialog
Replace your custom Button/Card with shadcn versions — inspect the generated code
Build: a product detail modal using the Dialog component

days.resource: Resource: ui.shadcn.com — click any component to see the install command and copy-paste code

tasks.text: Learn: skeleton loading — show placeholder shapes while data loads (not a spinner)
Add: npx shadcn@latest add skeleton
Build: ProductCardSkeleton — mirrors shape of ProductCard, shows during isLoading
Build: an ErrorFallback component for when queries fail — with a retry button
Test: throttle network in DevTools to Slow 3G — confirm skeletons appear

days.label: Day 4 · Thursday
days.title: Toast notifications + form with React Hook Form

tasks.text: Add: npx shadcn@latest add toast sonner
Show success toast after adding product, error toast on API failure
Install: npm install react-hook-form
Learn: useForm(), register(), handleSubmit(), formState.errors
Rewrite add-product form with React Hook Form — cleaner, less boilerplate
Add validation rules inside register() — required, minLength, pattern

days.resource: Resource: react-hook-form.com — "Get Started" page. It replaces all the manual onChange state management for forms.

tasks.text: Learn: React.memo — prevents re-render of child if props haven't changed
Learn: useCallback — memoize a function reference to pass as stable prop
Learn: lazy() + Suspense — code-split pages so they load on demand
Apply lazy() to all page components in your router — each becomes a separate chunk
Run: npm run build — inspect chunk sizes in the output to confirm splitting
Learn: useMemo — memoize an expensive computed value (e.g. filtered + sorted product list) so it only recalculates when its inputs change

weeks.askPrompt: Show me how to implement React.lazy and Suspense for code-splitting all my page components in React Router — with a loading fallback

weeks.title: Week 6 — Deploy to Vercel + project polish
weeks.description: Ship your frontend. Get it live, fast, and connected to your Phase 2 backend.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Environment variables + API config

tasks.text: Learn: Vite env vars must start with VITE_ to be exposed to the browser
Create .env.local — VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080/api/v1
Create src/api/axios.js — Axios instance with baseURL from import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL
Add request interceptor — automatically attach Authorization: Bearer {token} header
Add response interceptor — redirect to /login on 401 response
Add .env.local to .gitignore — never commit it

days.resource: This Axios setup (interceptors) is what Phase 4 is built on — your mock data will just be swapped for real API calls to your Railway backend.

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Deploy to Vercel

tasks.text: Push your React project to a GitHub repo
Create Vercel account at vercel.com — import your GitHub repo
Set environment variable in Vercel dashboard: VITE_API_URL = your Railway backend URL
Deploy — Vercel auto-detects Vite, builds, and gives you a live URL
Fix: add vercel.json with rewrites for SPA routing (client-side routes return 404 without it)
Test live URL in browser — confirm pages load and navigation works

days.resource: vercel.json fix: {"rewrites": [{"source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/"}]} — add this to project root to fix direct URL access on React Router routes.

tasks.text: Learn: semantic HTML — use button for actions, a for links, nav, main, section
Learn: alt text on all img tags — describe the image, not just "image"
Learn: aria-label for icon-only buttons — screen readers read this text
Learn: keyboard navigation — Tab through your app, every interactive element reachable?
Install axe DevTools Chrome extension — run it on your app, fix flagged issues
Install ESLint + Prettier — configure them if not already set up by Vite
Run ESLint — fix any warnings (unused variables, missing keys, etc.)
Review: each component does one job — split any that feel too large
Review: no hardcoded strings or magic numbers — use constants
Write README.md — project description, screenshots, setup steps, live URL
Install Vitest + React Testing Library (npm install -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/user-event jsdom) and write one test that renders ProductCard and checks its name/price
Final check: run through all pages on mobile, tablet, desktop viewports
Run Lighthouse audit (Chrome DevTools) — aim for 90+ on Performance and Accessibility
Note the Vercel URL — in Phase 4 you'll add it to Spring Boot CORS allowed origins
Preview Phase 4: read about how Axios interceptors attach JWT to requests
Push clean final version to GitHub — frontend repo ready to showcase
Self-assessment: without notes, build a component with useState + a useQuery hook, set up a nested route with a layout, and write an AuthContext with login/logout/persistence

weeks.askPrompt: Show me the Axios instance setup in React with request and response interceptors — attaching JWT token to every request and redirecting to login on 401

weeks.title: Phase 3 project — product storefront UI
weeks.description: A complete, live React frontend — ready to wire to your Spring Boot API in Phase 4.

projectCards.heading: What you'll build
projectCards.text: A responsive e-commerce storefront with product browsing, search, filters, a working cart, login/register pages, protected admin area, and skeleton loading — all powered by mock data (JSON) ready to swap for the real API.

projectCards.heading: Pages
projectCards.text: HomePage (hero + featured products) · ProductsPage (grid + search + filter + pagination) · ProductDetailPage (image, description, add to cart) · CartPage (items, qty controls, total) · LoginPage · RegisterPage · AdminProductsPage (protected, list + add + delete)

tasks.text: React Router with nested layout (Navbar + Footer on all pages)
TanStack Query for all data fetching — custom hooks for each resource
AuthContext — login/logout/persist session, verified with /auth/me on load
CartContext with useReducer — add, remove, update quantity
Protected route — /admin redirects to /login if not authenticated
Skeleton loading on all data-fetching pages
shadcn/ui components — Button, Card, Dialog, Badge, Toast
Fully responsive — mobile, tablet, desktop with Tailwind
React Hook Form on all forms
Lazy-loaded pages with Suspense fallback
Live on Vercel with correct SPA routing
Axios instance with interceptors ready for Phase 4
At least one component test with Vitest + React Testing Library

weeks.tip: A live, responsive React storefront with auth, cart, and admin — deployed on Vercel — is a serious portfolio piece. In Phase 4 you swap the mock data for your real Spring Boot API and the whole thing becomes one complete app.
weeks.askPrompt: Help me start building the Phase 3 project — a React e-commerce storefront. Start with the folder structure, React Router setup with nested layout, and the first two pages.
